How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-15 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers firing on 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, using cross-signal agreement to concentrate probability.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

ELV and CVS tie at 219, both carrying CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and a flow or volume signal with no mixed signals, while MS at 184 spreads across five screeners for the widest agreement in the batch. No ticker has a matched_combo, so none align with the validated SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 pair or the 81.7% win-rate three-screener bucket. Score_stats provides only n_overlaps, but the visible range runs 100 to 219 across 10 names, top-heavy at the ELV/CVS/MS tier with a clear step down after CAT at 183. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 strips the tailwind and keeps these setups conditional rather than high-conviction. Take MS first on breadth of screener agreement, at moderate conviction, with the absent matched_combo acting as a ceiling on sizing.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: BLK (score 6, target ~$1012.08 (DD20d -7.2%)).

5 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Flags unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day open-interest delta, filtered through EMA 8/21 trend structure and low-OI anomaly to isolate informed positioning.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$707.60 -1.41%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 4 resolved trades out of 282 total against a validity threshold of 20, the live 100% win rate and 200.0% average return are statistically void and confirm nothing about the backtest's 68.1% win rate, while monthly cohorts from April through June show universally 100% win rates on cohort sizes of 116, 146, and 9 that are almost entirely unresolved - the key number here is 4, and it means the live record does not yet exist in any meaningful sense.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (4 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 282 signals captured · 4 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.3%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 282 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's output is a single name: QQQ at score 50, Watch tier, flagging a bullish sweep. With zero signals above 65 and the median sitting at the absolute floor of the scoring range, this is the thinnest batch this screener can produce. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 adds no directional conviction to an already marginal read. QQQ as a first-look LONG does not clear the bar for active positioning today, and the batch should be treated as noise until a broader set of names surfaces with scores in the high tier.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms rather than contradicts the backtest - 80% win rate and +3.8% avg vs SPY's -0.1% is consistent with the Sharpe 2.34 OOS result, and monthly cohorts reinforce this with win rate recovering from April's 50.8% trough to June's 82.5%, pointing to early-year tape conditions as the drag rather than screener deterioration.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CL leads the batch at 78 with no pattern flag, making it the cleanest short-vol candidate; PCG at 77 and the SO, AEP, EXC, XEL, NEE cluster at 75-76 reflect the Sinclair +31% utility structural bias saturating the top of today's list. Score distribution is compressed - all 41 signals clear 65 and not one reaches 80, so there are no outliers to size up aggressively and no obvious names to skip. NEUTRAL macro at 51.75 is benign for premium selling but provides no regime tailwind, meaning the carry mechanics are doing the work, not a volatility collapse setup. Take CL first at the top score, giving sector diversification away from the utility block that dominates the rest of the batch.

🧠 Selects tickers where IV sits coherently above realized vol, avoiding disbelief-cheap and catalyst-rich extremes to capture a stable, uncontested variance premium.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

IONQ at 100 and HCA at 99 lead a batch of 8 names, all scored Very strong or Strong, all LONG, all pattern-null, so the signal is purely vol-regime based with no price-action overlay. The score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals print at or above 80 and the median sits at 75, meaning compression toward the high end rather than a broad spread. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 is benign but not a tailwind, which suits a vol-structure trade that does not require directional fuel. First preference is HCA over IONQ at moderate conviction: HCA is a large-cap healthcare operator with more anchored options pricing than a speculative quantum-computing name, and the 99 score with sector defensibility makes it the better risk-adjusted entry.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

ADMA, ALL, and CTRI each print 100 (Very strong, LONG), but so does every name in the batch - all 50 signals hit the ceiling with zero score variance and pattern is null across the board, leaving no internal ranking. A batch where median and max both equal 100 and every signal sits at or above 80 is ceiling-saturated; the screener is not discriminating today. Macro registers NEUTRAL at 51.75, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for the Kerberos hedging thesis. With no differentiator between names, trim position size; if forced to pick first, lean ADMA, but conviction is low.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tracking KILL (44% WR, avg -2.6% vs SPY -0.0%) contradicts the backtest's 65% win rate at the headline level, but the Very strong tier's live avg of +4.1% and the monthly cohort recovery from February's -3.3% through June's +2.7% on 15,671 signals suggest the KILL is a Signal-tier drag problem, not a screener-level failure.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 44% · avg -2.6% vs SPY -0.0% (-2.6 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 489 resolved at +20d · WR 44% · avg -2.6% · vs SPY -0.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 29% · 17% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MGM at a perfect score of 100 is the clear lead, with ARCB and BMA each at 90 filling out the Very strong tier. Score distribution is compressed hard to the ceiling: all 50 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 81, leaving no lower-tier names to filter out and no dispersion to use as a secondary ranking tool. NEUTRAL macro at 51.75 is neither a headwind nor a catalyst for reversal setups. The overall tracking KILL verdict is a real flag, but tier-level live data shows the Very strong cohort ran at 59.3% WR and +4.1% avg over 20 days, which is the directly comparable reference for today's picks. MGM first at half Kelly (roughly 10% of risk capital), sized below backtest allocation until the aggregate live track improves.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NNE and SMR both print 92, the session high, both Very Strong tier - two nuclear names moving together means this is a thematic cluster, not diversification. SWKS at 91 is the sole semis name in the top tier and offers a different sector angle without the correlated nuclear exposure. Score distribution is top-heavy in the right way: all 36 signals clear 65, 11 clear 80, median sits at 74, meaning there is no weak tail diluting the batch today. NEUTRAL macro at 51.75 is neither a catalyst nor a blocker for a mean-reversion basket, as this screener does not require a macro tailwind to work. First preference is SWKS at moderate conviction to avoid doubling the nuclear theme; NNE and SMR work as a paired basket add only if nuclear is already underweight in the book.

🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax using VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag peak-fear sell-vol entries.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$750.89 -0.52%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape earns its KEEP verdict at 70% WR and avg +1.4% versus SPY's +1.4% baseline, but the key number is the Signal/Watch split - Signal tier posted 89% WR and +2.35% avg on 9 trades while monthly average returns compressed from +5.4% in April to +0.64% in June, which partially contradicts backtest confidence and marks the edge as tier-dependent and decaying into summer.

🧠 Options volume 5x the 20-day mean, premium above $50K, Vol/OI above 5 in calm non-extended names - the mechanism is informed positioning before a catalyst.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.
Top picks: $MAT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikeDTEPremiumVol/OIIVDeltaTimingFlowOIToxicity
1MAT ↑53 Watch$14.41 +4.00%BEARISH SWEEPPUT1491$1258K47.0043.9%-0.38CONTRARIAN2140.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape shows 100% win rate across 15 resolved signals averaging 198.5% above SPY's 0.16% baseline, but the WATCH verdict exists because only 15 cases resolved against a 20-minimum threshold, and the backtest Bonferroni p of 0.097 keeps the edge MARGINAL, so the monthly cohort 100% win rates confirm nothing yet.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SPY leads at score 80 with a BEARISH SWEEP flagged alongside a LONG direction call - put flow sweeping the index in a NEUTRAL macro (regime score 51.75) reads as institutional hedging, not an outright directional long, and that contradiction undercuts the signal's actionability at face value. CZR scores 65 with a clean BULLISH SWEEP and is the strongest outright-long thesis in today's batch. EQT (69, Signal, HEDGE) shows defensive institutional positioning in energy but a HEDGE pattern is not a catalyst play. Score distribution is thin - median 54, only 1 name above 80 and 4 above 65 across 15 signals - and the NEUTRAL macro offers no directional tailwind to cover for it. CZR is first preference at moderate conviction; avoid forcing SPY given the directional ambiguity.

🧠 Scores 11 GICS sector ETFs on strength, acceleration, and RS crossover vs SPY to flag sectors shifting momentum regimes before the tape reprices them.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC and XLE are the only names worth attention, both printing 95 (Very strong, LONG) with nothing else above 65. XLF at 65 (Signal) is the sole mid-tier entry; XLRE, XLY, XLK, XLP, and XLI are all clustered at 50 to 55, which is Watch-tier noise with no pattern label to differentiate them. The distribution is sharply bifurcated: two names at max, one in the middle, five at the floor, with a hard gap between 65 and 95. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 51.75, a tepid backdrop that caps conviction on any sector rotation trade requiring a clear directional impulse. No pattern labels are populated for any ticker, which strips context on acceleration phase, so XLC gets the first look given its historical beta characteristics, but sizing should stay disciplined until regime climbs above 60.

🧠 Finds stocks off 40% from 52-week highs with capitulation volume and reversal candle, scoring drawdown depth, ATR contraction, and volume spike for a 15-day bounce.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

49ELME ↑71● Signal$1.55 +2.98%71.1315.2%110.15

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 51.7% win rate and +4.52% average beating SPY's 0.38% confirms real edge and supports the KEEP verdict despite the MARGINAL backtest label, and the monthly cohort climb from January's 29.3% win rate to June's 57.7% makes clear the edge is regime-sensitive and is currently on the right side of that dependency.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SOC is the one name to care about: a perfect score of 100 and the only Very Strong tier pick today, a tier that posts 57.7% win rate and +4.70% average in 90-day live tracking. The next cluster - LVWR at 88, CLB and MMS at 83, GLOB at 82 - all sit in the Strong tier, which is paradoxically the worst live performer at 37.8% win rate and -0.49% average, a fact that undercuts their apparent ranking. Score distribution is clustered and thin at the top: all 50 signals clear 65 but only 5 breach 80, median at 71, with little spread where it matters. The neutral macro regime at 51.75 provides no directional tailwind but does not veto the setup. Lead with SOC at high conviction and treat the Strong tier names as low priority until that tier's live record repairs itself.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on annualized 90-day exponential slope times R-squared, filtering for trend cleanliness above MA100 with a SPY-over-MA200 regime gate.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 Detects post-earnings continuation by scanning for follow-through bars within 5 days of a surprise, scored on short-term momentum and relative volume.
Construction
Tactical 5-day post-earnings continuation: scans for follow-through bars 1-5 days after surprise. Score from short-term momentum + relative volume.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M
  • Fixed 5-day hold
Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian LONG on SPY when two or more major intermarket correlation pairs break their 7-day 2-sigma band simultaneously.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Catches stocks gapping up on a positive EPS surprise and holding that gap, scoring continuation on surprise magnitude, gap size, and revision activity.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $AEHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1AEHR ↑83●● Strong$82.79 -5.70%1566.7%215.0%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live record at 55.7% WR and +3.5% avg beating SPY by 3.1 pts confirms the KEEP verdict, but the June 2026 cohort dropping to 37% WR and -0.64% average is the one number that flags the drift trade running cold into the current tape.

🧠 Detects institutional options footprints on low-activity stocks, using next-day OI confirmation to isolate new directional bets from closing flow.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Monthly cohorts through June flashed positive averages (+9.2% in June, +2.5% in May, +8.4% in April), but 90-day live tracking at 52.95% win rate and -2.6% average against SPY at +0.68% directly contradicts them, and the -3.3 point spread that drives the KILL verdict cannot be offset by a solo Sharpe of ~3.4 that was never block-bootstrapped.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 451 closed · WR TB 39% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

The score distribution is broad rather than top-heavy: all 50 signals clear 65, median is 77, and 17 are at or above 80. No pattern tags are present on any name, removing a layer of confirmation that would otherwise help distinguish noise from intent. Macro lands at NEUTRAL (51.75), offering no directional amplifier. Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

All 50 signals score 100 with median at the ceiling, so the screener offers zero discrimination today - every name lands in 'Very strong' tier and all pattern fields are null. BA is the only index-weight name in the visible picks; AFK and ASMB add breadth but give no incremental signal versus the rest of the batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 provides no directional tailwind. Critically, live tracking shows 'Very strong' tier posts 45.4% WR and -1.30% avg return, the worst of all three live tiers, meaning today's batch occupies exactly the cohort that loses money in the real tape. This is a weak batch; BA is the first look if forced, but conviction is floor.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms KEEP - WR 61% and avg +5.0% beats SPY by 3.1 points - but the tier inversion is the number to watch: Very Strong names average -2.6% while Signal-tier names average +7.7%, meaning the highest composite scores have actively predicted worse outcomes over this window, and June cohort improvement (+5.2% avg) has not changed that pattern.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ANAB at 95 and BV at 90 lead the batch as Very Strong, but the 90-day tier breakdown is a direct warning: that tier posts a 36% win rate and a -2.6% average, the worst of any tier in this screener. ABX and ACHC, both at 85 in the Strong tier, sit in a cohort that at least clears SPY, and they are the cleaner first look. The score distribution is compressed and top-heavy, with 48 of 50 signals clearing 65 and a median of 70, suggesting the screener is not being selective today. A neutral macro regime at 51.75 neither supports nor kills squeeze setups, so this batch lives or dies on stock-specific catalyst timing. Preference is ABX or ACHC first, reduced size, given the tier inversion at the top.

🧠 Ranks Russell 1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum to surface near-term leadership.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FHN, GTLS, and PYPL print at the maximum score of 100 today, with COMP, CTAS, DINO, GXO, and NU stacked at 99 - none carry a pattern tag, so the signal is pure rank on volume times momentum with no supplemental confirmation. The entire 50-signal batch sits between a median of 95 and a max of 100, the most compressed, top-heavy distribution this screen can produce, with zero score gradient to rank names against each other. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 does not actively oppose momentum, but it offers no tailwind either. Given the KILL verdict on live tracking, conviction is low across the board - if a position must be taken, PYPL at score 100 is the first name, minimum size only.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings drift by buying the weekly breakout after a red-candle pullback within 4 weeks of an earnings gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%

Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects mean-reversion setups where rejection tails of 1.8x ATR or more form at Market Profile value-area extremes, the only filter configuration that backtested positive.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by skipping KVO crossover and Force Index flip windows, exploiting the observation that volume-noise periods suppress win rate from 67% to 46%.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 offers no directional tailwind for retail flow. At Watch tier and a sub-65 score, XRT is a monitor, not an action - wait for a rescore above 65 before sizing any position.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking decisively contradicts the backtest: 90-day WR of 37% and avg return of -7.1% against SPY's -0.8% is a 6.3-point underperformance gap, and the June 2026 cohort at 30.8% WR and -7.3% avg shows the deterioration accelerating rather than mean-reverting, making the KILL verdict the load-bearing number here.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The three highest-scoring names are GGLS (100, Very strong), DRIP (98, Very strong), and TZA (92, Very strong), but DRIP is a leveraged inverse crude ETF, TZA is a 3x inverse small-cap fund, and TECS and SOXS further down the list are inverse tech and semi vehicles. A LONG breakout screener crowning four inverse ETFs in its top eight tells you the tape's strongest momentum today belongs to the bears, not to accumulation in healthy equity bases. Score distribution is severely top-heavy and thin: median score of 11 across 49 signals, only 3 names above 80, and zero pattern data available for any pick. NEUTRAL macro (51.75) provides no structural tailwind to offset this. Pass the batch; if forced to one name, GGLS at 100 is the only non-inverse pick in the top three, but the absence of pattern confirmation and the Very strong tier's own live record of 33% WR and -8.5% avg keep conviction at the floor.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Flags US equities where weekly RSI(14) drops below 30 then daily candlestick reversal fires inside a scored demand zone, targeting mean-reversion bounces.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $TDS $CAPR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern
1TDS ↑58 Watch$34.34 +0.75%31.2218.84YESENGULFING
2CAPR ↑57 Watch$20.15 -0.98%6.5329.80NOHAMMER
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The backtest is doctrine-rejected with all quantitative stats null, so the 90d live tape is the only evidence base, and it does hold up - 54% WR and +4.2% avg beats SPY by 3.3 points across 496 resolved trades - but the May cohort's avg of -0.44% despite a 56% WR is a warning that recent tail losses are quietly cutting into the edge the headline number advertises.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven mega-session 2026-05-16 crypto battery: 0/16 signals survive adversarial validation post-spot-ETF 2024 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and OI delta in crypto-linked ETFs to front-run informed positioning ahead of spot crypto moves.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MSTR ↑55 Watch$94.08 -3.48%45.34-22.8%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 Maps emerging investment themes by pulling tickers from qualifying theme ETFs and flagging each with a lifecycle status from EMERGING to CROWDED.
Construction

VOX leads the batch at 72, the only name above 70 across 50 signals, with HACK and IDNA both at 69, putting cybersecurity and genomics at the top of a communications-heavy cluster. The score distribution is compressed: median sits at 58, six names clear 65, and nothing breaks 80, which means the strongest name in the batch would be a mid-tier signal on any rigorous screener. Every top pick is an ETF and pattern fields are null across the board, stripping out any technical confirmation layer. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 offers no directional tailwind to lean on. VOX is the first look on score precedence alone, at low conviction, and the rest of the batch does not invite chasing.

Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest to anchor against, the 90-day live tape is the sole evidence base, and it holds: 64.9% win rate and a +7.9% average 20-day return beat SPY by 8.8 points over 131 resolved trades, though the only monthly cohort available - May 2026 - posts a 43.3% average that is inflated by a 1385% outlier and should not be read as repeatable.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 37% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The standout is SRTY, a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF, scoring 100 alongside seven equity longs - its inclusion as a LONG signal implies the screener is detecting small-cap relative weakness, not just broad market strength. CELH at 100 carries the most individual equity volatility of the batch and is the cleaner momentum read, while HDV at 100 adds a defensive dividend tilt that sits coherently with a NEUTRAL macro score of 51.75. All 8 top picks share the maximum score and Very strong tier with null patterns across the board, which removes any intra-batch ranking and limits differentiation to ticker-level judgment. The 50-signal batch is broad rather than top-heavy - median 70, 41 of 50 above 65, only 12 above 80 - so the 8 perfect-score names are a genuine cluster at the ceiling. CELH is the first preference for individual equity exposure, at moderate conviction given absent pattern context and a neutral tape.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals in today's batch score exactly 100, median included, so the screener offers no intra-batch ranking - every name is ceiling-out and equally weighted by construction. META and ASML are the most institutionally legible names, representing hedge fund accumulation in mega-cap tech and semiconductor capital equipment respectively, though both are deeply covered and the 13F lag means this reflects positioning from 60-90 days ago. MKSI is the most idiosyncratic pick, a smaller semis name where a new or increased position from a top fund would carry more signal weight, but pattern data is absent for all picks, limiting any single-name thesis. The flat score distribution paired with a NEUTRAL macro regime (51.75) argues against aggressive positioning across the batch. META is the first choice given liquidity and the breadth of institutional interest it likely reflects, but conviction is low.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FINS prints a perfect 100 and is the first name to look at, though pattern data is null across the entire batch, so the buy rationale is purely the rank-times-value signal. AVO and SVRE both score 99 and round out the top tier, with JEF at 98 worth noting as the most liquid name in the group. The score distribution is almost useless for prioritization: all 43 signals score at or above 80 and the median sits at 95, meaning the screener is handing out near-perfect scores uniformly and offering no internal gradient to lean on. NEUTRAL macro at 51.75 is neither a clear tailwind nor a headwind for insider-buy plays, but it does mean any position here is a pure stock-specific bet with no regime lift. Start with FINS at low conviction given the null pattern and compressed scoring.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks above $200M cap by EV/EBIT ascending, isolating the cheapest operating earners outside financials and utilities where valuation distortion is highest.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest completed there is nothing to confirm or contradict, and the 90-day live sample is too thin to compensate: 5 resolved trades, 20% win rate, average return of -0.06% against SPY's +1.36%, with one monthly cohort that simply repeats the same five observations.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 37 closed · WR TB 51% · 5% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

BZ at 100 and WRD at 97 lead today, both Very strong tier, no pattern overlay on either name. KROS at 93 rounds out the top three, same tier, same absence of a pattern catalyst. 🧠 Detects clusters of 3+ insider Form 4 buys within 30 days, weighted by rank and dollar size, signaling informed conviction ahead of price moves.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $CTM $FNWD $GLOO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value
1CTM ↑60 Watch$0.64 +1.73%4✓✓$1K

11PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.70 -4.24%3✓·$244K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no formal backtest to compare against, the live 90-day tape stands alone: 62.5% win rate and average +5.6% versus SPY at -0.4% across 464 resolved trades, with the June cohort (59.5% WR, +6.9% avg) and May cohort (64% WR, +4.9% avg) holding steady, which supports the KEEP verdict on internal consistency alone - the key number is the +6.1 point SPY spread.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum: absolute 12-month return positive and top relative rank among peers; long the leaders.

Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies top relative-strength tickers inside bullish sectors, scoring on 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.75 -1.19%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$144.88 +0.02%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$26.00 +0.31%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$134.25 -1.96%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$4.05 +2.14%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$81.66 -1.70%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the screener earns its keep - 57% win rate, +4.4% avg against SPY at -1.7%, a +6.1-point edge across 180 resolved trades - and the June cohort extends that with 100% WR on 5 trades, though the May best-of-1149% distorts the monthly avg and should carry no weight in sizing expectations.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Six signals today with a thin top end: only ABX clears 80, ACA at 68 is the only other name above 65, and four names pile into Watch territory between 55 and 64. ABX leads on raw score but sits in the Strong tier, which historically returns just +1.9% avg on a 49% win rate - the weakest tier outcome in the 90-day tracking set. ACA at 68 in the Signal tier is the more compelling setup, as Signal historically posts 73% win rate and +9.4% avg, easily the best tier by both metrics. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 51.75) is not a tailwind for sector rotation, which wants a clear directional trend, so overall batch conviction is moderate. First preference is ACA at moderate conviction; ABX is secondary given tier-level underperformance despite the higher score.

C (72) and BX (71) are the next-best Signal names, both financials, adding sector concentration to the short thesis. Seven of eight names score 65 or above with a median of 67, so the distribution is compressed and top-heavy rather than broadly dispersed, with no meaningful bench below DHI. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.75 is not a tailwind for aggressive put positioning, and the absence of pattern tags on every name means the signal is score-driven with no additional structural confirmation. DHI first at moderate conviction, C secondary.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Flags stocks lagging their sector during LATE_CHASE or RIDING phases, scoring by sector relative strength times individual under-performance to capture catch-up mean reversion.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Pattern data is null across all five names, removing the technical confirmation that normally sharpens a catch-up entry. 🧠 Identifies retail euphoria by combining social mention z-score, call/put spike, and parabolic price stretch into a composite short signal absent any catalyst.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AAP $ALB $CELC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AAP ↓84●● Strong$53.36 +2.44%EUPHORIA0.84-6.0%
2ALB ↓64 Watch$120.79 -3.17%GREED0.64-3.1%
3CELC ↓54 Watch$88.81 -2.95%GREED0.54-17.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where IV30 exceeds HV20 by more than 1.5 standard deviations, identifying elevated fear premiums as candidates for options premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $BMRN $LLY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1BMRN ↓62 Watch$59.26 +0.53%42%27%1.56FEAR
2LLY ↓62 Watch$1152.32 -0.37%51%33%1.55FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists to confirm or deny - N=25 remains below the required 30-trade threshold - and live tracking at 43% win rate with +1.1% average return matches SPY to within rounding, with monthly cohorts worsening from April's 56% win rate and +10.8% average to June's 33% win rate and -3.6% average, making the key number June's -3.6% cohort drift.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags daily closes outside Value Area boundaries that reverse next bar, trapping breakout traders and generating mean-reversion entries opposite the trap direction.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AEO $AVDV
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AEO ↑75● Signal$17.58 +0.98%BEAR_TRAP16.0117.930.80
2AVDV ↑60 Watch$103.91 -0.96%BEAR_TRAP103.71105.571.50
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Fifty-six resolved trades at 68% WR and +5.3% avg beat over SPY's near-zero 90-day return, with May and June cohorts running at 77% and 65% WR respectively, confirm the live tape backs the trap logic even though the formal backtest sits at N=0, and the 5.3-point SPY spread is the number that earns the KEEP.

Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $SVXY $UVXY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$58.34 -0.83%28%720%RULE_1
2UVXY ↓85●● Strong$23.38 +2.95%81%960%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV crushes call IV above the 90th percentile, detecting unusual institutional put demand that may precede downside moves.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$378.79 -3.93%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day sample (9 trades, 55.6% win rate, avg -5.7% vs SPY's +0.77%) contradicts any edge thesis, with the two Strong-tier trades at 0% win rate and a -55.9% average return as the number that breaks the sample and keeps this firmly in WATCH, not trade, territory.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Finds pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, capturing volatility mispricing before the catalyst.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $HON
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1HON ↑65● Signal$224.26 +0.64%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HON is the sole signal today, scoring 65 - floor of the Signal tier - with no named pattern to anchor the thesis. A single-name batch at median 65 and zero names above 80 is as thin as a screener gets. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (51.75), neither a tailwind nor a headwind for vol mispricing plays. HON earns first look by default, not on strength, so size small and wait for pre-earnings vol contraction or price confirmation before committing.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bearish put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline and reads extreme hedging demand as a contrarian long entry when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑55 Watch$94.08 -3.48%66%960.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no completed backtest to anchor against, the 90-day live data (55% WR, +6.7% avg, beats SPY by 5.4 points) says KEEP, but the monthly cohort trend shows a worrying split: June's avg fell to -2.4% despite a 58% win rate, down sharply from May at +10.1% and April at +9.2%, meaning the key number is that recent winners are shrinking fast even as the hit rate holds.

🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress probability using Altman Z-score composite factors across profitability, leverage, liquidity, and activity ratios.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional footprints by reading price spread and volume relationships to locate supply and demand imbalances before directional moves.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
